diagrams.description: Labor Law (also known as employment law) governs the relationship between employers, employees, and trade unions. This flowchart outlines its framework: the ultimate goal is to balance employer needs with worker protection and social justice. It integrates individual employment law (contracts, conditions, termination, discrimination) and collective labor law (unions, bargaining, strikes). These are regulated through core principles (minimum standards, non‑discrimination, freedom of association, collective bargaining) and enforced via administrative agencies, labor courts, and dispute‑resolution mechanisms. The system produces outcomes such as fair wages, workplace safety, industrial peace, and social dialogue.
